what: THE ASSEMBLY POINT. A compiled view — the system-scale index projected over `intentions-other/` PLUS the charters homed beside the code in cairn/. Nothing is authored here. This directory holds this charter and, once the projector exists, compiled output; a hand-written intention in this folder is a defect, not a contribution.
why:
Akien, 2026-07-22: 'the CairnCommons intentions folder becomes the place the other intentions are assembled into.' This is the outer row of the three-scale projector recursion — ticket (history -> state window), component (a component's intentions + tickets -> its charter as summarized design), system (all sources -> here). Same move each time: compile a bounded view over an append-only source so no mind re-derives it by reading everything (Law 1).

What it is FOR is the seed claim: take this one folder to a bare machine and regrow the system. That only works if the view is compiled over BOTH sources — the beside-code charters alone regrow a repo that does not run, because everything that made a machine ready to run it lives in `intentions-other/`.
the_roots_surface_first: `telos.md` and `core-values.md` are the oldest records in `intentions-other/` (both 2026-07-14, the day the repo was founded), so a timestamp-ordered compile puts the frame at the top of the scan by physics rather than by a special case (Akien, 2026-07-22 — Law 4). This is why there is no separate `values/` container: splitting the roots into a sibling folder would have broken the seed claim, since carrying this folder to a bare machine would have left the frame behind.
no_hand_edits: A compiled view is never hand-edited (CLAUDE.md, 'Rules awaiting physics'). The only door is: change a source in `intentions-other/` or a charter beside its code, then recompile. IOU, and an honest one — the projector for this scale does NOT exist yet, so today the rule is prose and the folder is empty by convention rather than closed by physics. The charter-scale projector that proves the pattern is built and green (`cairn/charter/projector.py`); this scale is not. Until it lands, a hand-written file here is caught by a reader, which is exactly the discipline-instead-of-physics that Cairn exists to stop relying on.
owner: the store owns the collection; the projector (unbuilt) will be the sole writer. No per-record owner here — a compiled record's owner is the owner of the source it came from (Law 6 travels with the source, not the projection).
template: not applicable — records here are projections, shaped by the projector, not authored against a template. The template that matters is the one in `intentions-other/_charter+why.json`, which governs the sources.
notes_on_the_store: Emptiness is the correct resting state until the projector exists. An empty compiled view is honest; a hand-maintained one that LOOKS compiled is the drift this whole design is built to make impossible.

The middle row of the recursion stays authored on purpose: a component's charter is the precipitate of design conversation, not a compilation of its tickets. Whether that ever changes is deliberately open (Law 3, guess and adjust).
provenance: Emptied and re-chartered 2026-07-22, when the last hand-authored files left for `intentions-other/` — the four I-*.md spanning intentions plus the two roots. Akien: 'that's why I said they'd go in intentions-other.' The folder's job changed from holding intentions to assembling them.
